title: How a week of news tastes like a soggy cabbage sandwich: Alaska News Brief March 2023
summary: I took some time off to see my family in early March. Within a day of returning, it felt like I hadn’t left at all. Instead, I felt like someone delivered a soggy old cabbage sandwich made up of somewhat good news stuffed with clearly bad news. I’m as hungry for good news as anyone, but this dish ta...

title: Pilgrim Hot Springs Recruiting for Cultural Advisory Committee
summary: Kawerak, Inc. is a co-managing owner of Pilgrim Hot Springs. On behalf of Unaatuq, LLC., Kawerak is working to assemble a Cultural Advisory Committee, comprised of people having a direct or indirect relationship with the Pilgrim Hot Springs mission. The committee meetings will allow Pilgrim Hot Springs staff the ability to gain input on culturally relevant t...

title: COVID-19 vaccinations and boosters available by appointment only starting March 20
summary: Effective Monday, March 20, the walk-in COVID-19 clinic on the third floor of the hospital in Bethel will be closing—but getting a COVID-19 vaccination or booster is still easy! In Bethel, call 907-543-6442 to schedule a time for your shot. In villages, call your local clinic. YK Delta residents ove...

title: When the Secretary withdrew a land exchange that traded away vital wetlands in Izembek Refuge, did it put this issue to bed? Not yet.
summary: Secretary of the Interior Deb Haaland announced on March 14 that the department would withdraw from the land exchange between the Interior Department and King Cove Corporation authorized in July 2019. This is great news. It means that Izembek National Wildlife Refuge remains protected and intact--fo...
